notes.anchor: on the sixth day before the Kalends
notes.note: Romans counted days inclusively backwards from three fixed points each month: the Kalends (1st), Nones (5th or 7th), and Ides (13th or 15th). The sixth day before the February Kalends is 24 January.

notes.anchor: the Senate decreed by name that he should at once go to Sicily
notes.note: Sicily, a grain-supplying province strategically vital in a civil war, needed a commander loyal to the Pompeian cause; the Senate by name (nominatim) ordered Postumius to relieve the outgoing governor Furfanius before a regular successor could be installed.

notes.anchor: He is being sent ahead to Sicily with imperium
notes.note: Imperium was the formal power of military command and jurisdiction held by senior magistrates and promagistrates; sending Fannius ahead with imperium gave him lawful authority to govern and defend the province in the emergency.

notes.anchor: the public money too, which is in the treasury
notes.note: The aerarium, the Roman state treasury, was housed in the temple of Saturn under quaestorian custody; the Pompeians' failure to secure it before evacuating Rome left its reserves to fall into Caesar's hands.
